extraction.legacyBehaviour:
Three near-identical legacy delete handlers in PatientResource.java perform the SAME reversal sequence, all gated PENDING-only and all HARD-deleting at the end:

LAB — deleteLabTest, PatientResource.java:2912-2965:
1. Load LabTest by id (2916). Guard: if status != "PENDING" throw InvalidOperationException "Could not delete, only a PENDING lab test can be deleted" (2917-2919). [Note: no isEmpty() check before .get() — NPE/NoSuchElement risk on bad id.]
2. Re-load the linked PatientBill via patientBillRepository.findById(labTest.getPatientBill().getId()).get() (2922).
3. Find PatientPaymentDetail by that bill (2924). If present AND its status == "RECEIVED" (2925): create a new PatientCreditNote with amount = pd.getPatientBill().getAmount() (FULL bill amount), patient = bill's patient, reference = "Canceled lab test", status = "PENDING", no = patientCreditNoteService.requestPatientCreditNoteNo().getNo(), plus createdBy/createdOn/createdAt; save it (2927-2938).
4. Find PatientInvoiceDetail by that bill (2940). If present: delete the invoice detail (2945); then count remaining details of the parent PatientInvoice using a BROKEN loop `int j=0; for(...){ j = j++; }` (2947-2950) — j++ post-increment makes `j = j++` a no-op, so j stays 0 — and `if(j==0) delete the parent PatientInvoice` (2951-2953). Because j is ALWAYS 0, the parent invoice is ALWAYS deleted whenever a detail existed (CR-10 legacy bug — corrupts multi-line claims).
5. If a PatientPaymentDetail was present (regardless of RECEIVED status) HARD-delete it (2955-2959). The "do not delete a paid test" guard is commented OUT (2956-2957).
6. HARD-delete the LabTest (2960) and HARD-delete the PatientBill (2961).
7. Return 201 body true (2963-2964).

RADIOLOGY — deleteRadiology, PatientResource.java:3418-3471: IDENTICAL structure. Guard message "Could not delete, only a PENDING radiology can be deleted" (3424). Credit-note reference = "Canceled radiology" (3436). Same RECEIVED guard (3431), same broken j=j++ invoice loop (3453-3459), same HARD-delete of payment detail (3461-3465), radiology (3466) and bill (3467).

PROCEDURE — deleteProcedure, PatientResource.java:3473-3537: IDENTICAL structure. Guard message "Could not delete, only a PENDING procedure can be deleted" (3479). Credit-note reference = "Canceled procedure" (3503). Same RECEIVED guard (3486), same broken j=j++ loop (3520-3526), same HARD-delete of payment detail (3528-3532), procedure (3534) and bill (3535). (An older commented-out credit-note block at 3490-3498 used reference "Procedure canceled" and no="NA" — DEAD CODE, NOT the active behaviour.)

PRESCRIPTION (sibling, for pattern reference) — deletePrescription, PatientResource.java:3540-3599+: guard allows PENDING OR NOT-GIVEN (3545); credit-note reference "Canceled prescription"; the refund branch is gated on pd status == "GIVEN" (3553) rather than "RECEIVED" — a notable divergence, not part of ITEM1's L/R/P trio but flagged for ITEM-completeness.

NET legacy reversal per delete: (a) PatientBill HARD-deleted; (b) PatientPaymentDetail HARD-deleted; (c) a PENDING PatientCreditNote for the FULL bill amount raised ONLY when a RECEIVED payment existed; (d) PatientInvoiceDetail deleted and parent PatientInvoice deleted (always, due to bug); (e) the clinical order row HARD-deleted. No refund-flip exists in legacy — the payment row is erased, not marked REFUNDED.

guards: Order status MUST equal 'PENDING' before any reversal runs; otherwise InvalidOperationException is thrown and nothing is mutated (PatientResource.java:2917-2919 / 3423-3425 / 3478-3480). NOTE legacy does NOT block a PAID bill at delete time — the 'cannot delete a paid test' check is commented out (2956-2957 / 3462-3463 / 3529-3530); a PENDING order whose bill happens to be PAID/RECEIVED still deletes, and that is precisely the case that raises the credit note.
Credit-note is raised ONLY if a PatientPaymentDetail exists AND its status == 'RECEIVED' (2925 / 3431 / 3486). If no payment detail, or it is not RECEIVED, NO credit note is created.
Credit-note amount = the bill amount (pd.getPatientBill().getAmount()), i.e. FULL refund of the bill (2928 / 3434 / 3501).
Credit-note number from patientCreditNoteService.requestPatientCreditNoteNo().getNo() (2932 / 3438 / 3505); status forced 'PENDING' (2931 / 3437 / 3504).

Invoice detach: delete PatientInvoiceDetail if present, then delete parent PatientInvoice — legacy ALWAYS deletes the parent due to the j=j++ no-op bug (2947-2953 / 3453-3459 / 3520-3526). As-built CORRECTLY deletes parent ONLY when zero details remain (CreditNoteService.java:177-189, CR-10 FIX) — this is a ratified, owner-approved deviation, NOT a parity defect to reproduce.

Order of operations in legacy: (1) status guard, (2) reload bill, (3) credit-note on RECEIVED, (4) invoice-detail delete + parent-invoice delete, (5) payment-detail hard-delete, (6) order hard-delete, (7) bill hard-delete. As-built cancelCharge order: (1) soft-cancel bill, (2) refund+credit-note, (3) detach invoice; the clinical service then deletes the order row.
DO NOT reproduce legacy HARD-delete of PatientBill/PatientPaymentDetail — per MEMORY/owner decision the soft-flag pattern (CANCELED bill, REFUNDED payment) in cancelCharge is the ratified standard.
Idempotency: a second cancelCharge on an already-CANCELED bill finds no RECEIVED payment and raises NO second credit note (CreditNoteService.java:113-114, doc 67-68).

(2) All verbatim message and reference strings match the source character-for-character. (3) The j=j++ no-op invoice-deletion bug is present verbatim at all three sites (CR-10). (4) The legacy 'paid test' guard is commented out (2956-2957 / 3462-3463 / 3529-3530), so a PENDING order with a RECEIVED-paid bill still deletes and raises the credit note. (5) The DEAD commented-out 'Procedure canceled'/no='NA' block at 3490-3498 is correctly identified as non-active. AS-BUILT verification: BillingCommands.cancelCharge IS published (BillingCommands.java:58); CreditNoteService.doCancel performs the full soft-flag reversal — bill.cancel() at :107, markRefunded + full-amount PENDING credit note at :147-167, detachInvoiceDetail with empty-only parent delete at :177-189 (CR-10 FIX); findReceivedByBillUid (PatientPaymentDetailRepository.java:24) and findByBillUid (PatientInvoiceDetailRepository.java:23) both exist. extraction.item: ITEM 2 — Radiology stand-alone bill-gated add_report (legacy POST /patients/radiologies/add_report)
extraction.legacyBehaviour: Legacy endpoint POST /patients/radiologies/add_report (PatientResource.java:3183-3197) is a controller-inline handler `void addReport(@RequestBody Radiology radiology, HttpServletRequest request)`. Flow, exactly: (1) Load Radiology by the body's id via radiologyRepository.findById(radiology.getId()) (PatientResource.java:3187). (2) If empty -> throw NotFoundException("Radiology not found") (3188-3190). (3) Bill-status gate (3191): if `r.get().getPatientBill().getStatus()` equals "PAID" OR "COVERED" OR "VERIFIED", then set the radiology's report to the request body's report (`r.get().setReport(radiology.getReport())`) and save (3192-3193). (4) ELSE throw InvalidOperationException("Could not add report. Payment not verified") (3194-3195). The method returns void on success (HTTP 200, no body). It writes ONLY the `report` field (Radiology.report, @Column(length=10000), Radiology.java:47-48) — it does NOT change the radiology order status, does NOT touch result/attachment/verified_* stamps, and records NO actor/day audit. ORDER STATUS IS NEVER CHECKED: there is no guard on r.get().getStatus(); report can be written at ANY radiology lifecycle status (PENDING/ACCEPTED/REJECTED/VERIFIED/COLLECTED) provided the bill gate passes. The gate is purely on the linked PatientBill.status (the @OneToOne PatientBill, Radiology.java:81-84). NOTE: the separate verifyRadiologyResult endpoint (PatientResource.java:4272-4295, ACCEPTED->VERIFIED) writes result+attachment but NEVER writes report (4283-4292), so add_report is the SOLE writer of Radiology.report in the legacy system (confirmed: no setReport anywhere in PatientServiceImpl.java; only add_report match in com.orbix.api). The lab equivalent addLabTestReport (PatientResource.java:3381-3395) is byte-for-byte identical in structure — same exact bill-status set {PAID,COVERED,VERIFIED}, same verbatim message, same no-order-status-check, differing ONLY in entity type (LabTest vs Radiology) and the not-found message ("Lab Test not found" vs "Radiology not found"). Both write only the report field, no status change.
states: Radiology order status is IRRELEVANT to add_report — NOT checked at PatientResource.java:3183-3197. Report may be written when the order is in any of: PENDING, ACCEPTED, REJECTED, VERIFIED, COLLECTED (full status set per setStatus calls PatientResource.java:4221/4246/4267/4292/4333).
The ONLY gate is the linked PatientBill.status, which must be in the set {PAID, COVERED, VERIFIED} (PatientResource.java:3191).

add_report does NOT mutate the radiology order status — no setStatus call in the handler (3183-3197); contrast with addProcedureNote which DOES set status VERIFIED (3410) and verifyRadiologyResult which sets VERIFIED (4292).
verbatimMessages: Radiology not found
Could not add report. Payment not verified
guards: Order of checks (PatientResource.java:3187-3196): (1) findById on body id; (2) NotFoundException("Radiology not found") if absent; (3) bill-status gate r.get().getPatientBill().getStatus() IN {"PAID","COVERED","VERIFIED"}; (4) on pass: setReport + save; on fail: InvalidOperationException("Could not add report. Payment not verified").

Bill-status string comparison is case-sensitive equals() on the literal set {PAID, COVERED, VERIFIED} (3191). Any other PatientBill.status value (e.g. UNPAID, PENDING, CANCELED, REFUNDED) falls through to the else branch and is rejected.

NO order-status precondition. NO empty/blank report-body check (contrast addProcedureNote 3405-3407 which rejects empty note with InvalidEntryException). NO actor/day stamping. NO membership / insurance-plan re-check.
PatientBill is a non-null @OneToOne (Radiology.java:81-84, optional=false, nullable=false) so getPatientBill() is assumed present; the handler does NOT null-guard it before reading getStatus().
extraction.asBuiltDelta:
ABSENT in as-built. RadiologyService (HMSCLEAN2 backend/src/main/java/com/otapp/hmis/clinical/application/RadiologyService.java) has NO addReport method at all. In the as-built model `report` is written only inside verify() (RadiologyService.java:301-316 -> r.verify(result, report, attachment,...)), gated on order status == ACCEPTED — which is NOT the legacy behaviour: legacy add_report is a separate report-only write at ANY order status, gated on bill status, and legacy verifyRadiologyResult never writes report. So the stand-alone bill-gated radiology add_report endpoint must be NEWLY ADDED in inc-06A.

CRITICAL BLOCKER — the gate cannot be reproduced with current seams: the legacy gate reads the linked PatientBill.status and requires it in {PAID, COVERED, VERIFIED}. Per the owner-confirmed ADR-0008 §6, clinical NEVER reads billing bill-status post-hoc; it holds only a local `settled` flag set at ORDER time (RadiologyService.java:148, isSettledFromCharge:581-587). That flag is NOT equivalent: (a) it is boolean, losing the PAID/COVERED/VERIFIED distinction; (b) it is captured at order creation and is NOT updated when a CASH-OPD bill is later paid (the cash-PAID->settled propagation is explicitly DEFERRED, RadiologyService.java:73). A bill paid AFTER the order was created would still show settled=false, so a settled-flag check would wrongly reject add_report that legacy would allow. The as-built billing.api.BillingCommands surface (billing/api/BillingCommands.java:22-74) exposes ONLY recordClinicalCharge, cancelCharge, approveInvoicesForBills — there is NO bill-status read seam. RECOMMENDATION: inc-06A must add a narrow read-only billing.api seam, e.g. BillingQueries.getBillStatus(billUid) -> BillStatus (or a boolean isReportReleasable(billUid) returning status IN {PAID,COVERED,VERIFIED}), called by clinical at report time, preserving the String-only / no-domain-type-crossing boundary (ADR-0008 §1). The radiology row already persists chargeResult.billUid() (RadiologyService.java:154), so the bill ULID is available to pass.

ADDITIONAL DISCREPANCY TO FLAG (lab, ITEM-adjacent): the as-built LabTestService.addReport (LabTestService.java:400-414) gates on ORDER status == COLLECTED with message "Please collect the lab test first" — this is NOT legacy parity. Legacy lab addLabTestReport (PatientResource.java:3389-3393) gates on BILL status {PAID,COVERED,VERIFIED} with message "Could not add report. Payment not verified", and never checks order status. The as-built lab DTO/javadoc (LabTestReportRequest.java; LabTestService.java:397-398) document the COLLECTED gate as if it were the rule. If ITEM 2/ITEM 4 require legacy-exact bill-gating, the existing lab addReport is a latent defect (wrong gate + wrong message) and should be corrected with the same bill-status seam. Both the radiology (new) and lab (correction) paths should share the seam.

extraction.item: ITEM3 — save_reason_for_rejection (lab + radiology): post-rejection edit of rejectComment, REJECTED-status-only guard
extraction.legacyBehaviour:
Two separate, parallel endpoints exist, both implemented INLINE in the controller (no service-layer delegation, no @Transactional annotation visible on the method) and both returning `void` (HTTP 200 with empty body on success):

LAB — POST /patients/lab_tests/save_reason_for_rejection (PatientResource.java:2034-2048), method saveLabTestRejectComment(@RequestBody LabTest test, HttpServletRequest request).
RADIOLOGY — POST /patients/radiologies/save_reason_for_rejection (PatientResource.java:2018-2032), method saveRejectComment(@RequestBody Radiology radiology, HttpServletRequest request).

Exact behaviour (identical shape for both, only entity/repo/labels differ):
1. Look up the entity by the id carried in the request body: labTestRepository.findById(test.getId()) / radiologyRepository.findById(radiology.getId()) (PatientResource.java:2038 / :2022). NOTE: the request body is deserialized into a full LabTest/Radiology entity but ONLY .getId() and .getRejectComment() are read; all other body fields are ignored.
2. If not present -> throw NotFoundException. Lab message: "Lab Test not found" (:2040); Radiology message: "Radiology not found" (:2024).
3. GUARD: if the PERSISTED entity's status equals the literal string "REJECTED" (r.get().getStatus().equals("REJECTED"), :2042 / :2026) -> set rejectComment from the request body (r.get().setRejectComment(test.getRejectComment()) / setRejectComment(radiology.getRejectComment()), :2043 / :2027) and save (:2044 / :2028).
4. ELSE (status is anything other than the exact string "REJECTED", including null) -> throw InvalidOperationException("Could not save. Only allowed for rejected tests") — the SAME message for BOTH lab and radiology (:2046 / :2030).

Field written: rejectComment (String) on LabTest (LabTest.java:134) and Radiology (Radiology.java:125). The guard reads status (String) on LabTest (LabTest.java:55) and Radiology (Radiology.java:51) — free-text String, NOT an enum; comparison is case-sensitive .equals("REJECTED").

CRITICAL COUPLING (verified): The legacy reject action itself (reject_lab_test PatientResource.java:3914-3937; reject_radiology :4226-4249) does NOT set rejectComment at all — it only flips status to "REJECTED" and stamps the rejected_* triplet, nulling the accepted_* triplet. Therefore save_reason_for_rejection is the ONLY code path in the legacy system that ever populates rejectComment. The intended workflow is: reject first (status->REJECTED, comment stays null), THEN call save_reason_for_rejection to attach/edit the reason. The comment can be re-saved/overwritten any number of times while status remains "REJECTED" (no idempotency/one-shot restriction). No null/blank validation on the incoming rejectComment — a null or empty reason is accepted and persisted when status is REJECTED.

HTTP wire format (via @ControllerAdvice ExceptionController.java): NotFoundException -> HTTP 404, body string "Not found: Lab Test not found" / "Not found: Radiology not found" (ExceptionController.java:17-20). InvalidOperationException -> HTTP 409 CONFLICT, body string "Invalid operation: Could not save. Only allowed for rejected tests" (ExceptionController.java:29-32). Note the handler PREFIXES the raw message; the raw guard message is the un-prefixed "Could not save. Only allowed for rejected tests".

FRONTEND (observable UX): Lab lab-test.component.ts:301-323 saveReasonForRejection(id, reason) POSTs body {id, rejectComment:reason}; on 2xx shows msgBox.showSuccessMessage('Success') (:315); on error shows the raw server error via showErrorMessage (:320). Triggered by lab-test.component.html:39 button bound to labTest.id + labTest.rejectComment (a free-text input, html:38). Radiology mirrors exactly: radiology.component.ts:178-? POSTs {id, rejectComment:reason} and shows 'Success' (:192). No client-side guard on status or on empty reason — the frontend sends regardless, relying entirely on the server guard.
states: LabTest/Radiology status is a free-text String (NOT enum): values seen across lifecycle include PENDING, ACCEPTED, REJECTED, COLLECTED (lab), VERIFIED (lab)
save_reason_for_rejection mutates NO status — it only writes the rejectComment field; status is read-only in this endpoint
Guard requires status == exact string "REJECTED" (case-sensitive .equals). Any other value, or null, fails the guard.

Reachable predecessor state: REJECTED is produced ONLY by reject_lab_test (:3914-3937) / reject_radiology (:4226-4249), which themselves leave rejectComment null. So at the moment REJECTED is first reached, rejectComment is null until this endpoint sets it.

accept (PatientResource.java accept_* paths) clears rejectComment back to null when moving REJECTED->ACCEPTED (as-built mirrors this at LabTest.java:455-459) — after which save_reason_for_rejection would again be blocked by the guard.

guards: Precondition 1 (both): entity must exist by request-body id, else NotFoundException (404). Lab: "Lab Test not found" (PatientResource.java:2040); Radiology: "Radiology not found" (:2024).

Precondition 2 (both, the core guard): persisted status.equals("REJECTED") — exact, case-sensitive String compare (:2042 / :2026). If true -> write rejectComment + save. If false (any other status incl. null) -> InvalidOperationException "Could not save. Only allowed for rejected tests" (409) (:2046 / :2030).
Order of checks: (1) findById/exists FIRST, then (2) status==REJECTED guard. A non-existent id yields 404, never the 409 guard message.
Status guard reads the PERSISTED entity status (r.get().getStatus()), NOT the status in the request body — body status is ignored.

1. NO endpoint equivalent to legacy save_reason_for_rejection. grep for save_reason/saveReason/saveRejectComment/reason_for_rejection/updateRejectComment across the whole backend returns ZERO matches. There is no way in inc-05 to EDIT/attach a reject comment AFTER rejection on an already-REJECTED order.

This is an inc-05 improvement that COLLAPSED two legacy steps into one. To restore exact parity, inc-06A must add a saveRejectComment-style operation that: (a) finds by uid (404 \"Lab Test not found\"/\"Radiology not found\"), (b) guards status==REJECTED else 422/409 with verbatim \"Could not save. Only allowed for rejected tests\", (c) sets only rejectComment and saves, (d) is re-callable while REJECTED. NOTE the legacy HTTP code for the guard failure is 409 (InvalidOperationException -> CONFLICT); inc-05 maps its InvalidPatientOperationException to 422 (per existing accept/reject docs at LabTestController.java:173,185) — confirm desired status code with engagement-lead, since this is an observable HTTP-status divergence.
4. Note: inc-05 has no equivalent of the legacy null/blank-allowed comment; decide whether to keep legacy's no-validation behaviour.

All of the following were confirmed EXACT: the two inline controller endpoints saveRejectComment (PatientResource.java:2018-2032) and saveLabTestRejectComment (:2034-2048) — both return void, no @Transactional, no @PreAuthorize, read only id+rejectComment from the body, findById-first then status.equals(\"REJECTED\") case-sensitive guard, setRejectComment+save on pass, InvalidOperationException(\"Could not save. The as-built delta is also verified: grep for save_reason/saveReason/saveRejectComment/reason_for_rejection/updateRejectComment across the whole backend returns ZERO matches (no post-rejection edit endpoint exists); there is no setRejectComment-while-REJECTED domain path (rejectComment is written only at LabTest.java:459 [accept->null] and :476 [reject]); inc-05 reject() sets the comment inline (LabTestService.java:300, LabTest.java:476), collapsing the legacy two-step; and the 409-vs-422 divergence is REAL (legacy InvalidOperationException->409; as-built InvalidPatientOperationException->422 via ErrorCode.BUSINESS_RULE, InvalidPatientOperationException.java:6/:34, LabTestController.java:173/:185). extraction.item: ITEM 4 — Post-VERIFIED report amendment asymmetry (lab & radiology add_report gated on bill status only, never order status)
extraction.legacyBehaviour:
CONFIRMED REAL. Both legacy `add_report` endpoints gate ONLY on the linked PatientBill status and NEVER read the order's own status field, so the free-text report/narrative is overwritable at ANY order lifecycle stage — including indefinitely AFTER the order reaches VERIFIED — whereas the structured result data is permanently frozen at verification.

LAB — addLabTestReport, PatientResource.java:3381-3395:
- Lookup only: findById, NotFoundException if empty (3385-3388). No order-status read.
- Single guard (3389): `if(r.get().getPatientBill().getStatus().equals("PAID") || ...equals("COVERED") || ...equals("VERIFIED"))`. This is BILL status (PatientBill.getStatus()), not LabTest.getStatus().
- On pass (3390-3391): `r.get().setReport(labTest.getReport()); labTestRepository.save(...)` — overwrites the `report` String field. No level/range/unit/result touched.
- On fail (3392-3393): throws InvalidOperationException("Could not add report. Payment not verified").
- THERE IS NO `r.get().getStatus()` CHECK ANYWHERE IN THE METHOD. The LabTest order status (PENDING/ACCEPTED/COLLECTED/VERIFIED) is never read, so a VERIFIED test's report can be overwritten as many times as desired, with no re-verification, no status change, and no amendment trail.

RADIOLOGY — addReport, PatientResource.java:3183-3197: byte-for-byte the same shape.
- Lookup + NotFoundException (3187-3190).
- Single guard (3191): identical PatientBill-status check (PAID|COVERED|VERIFIED).
- On pass (3192-3193): `r.get().setReport(radiology.getReport()); radiologyRepository.save(...)`.
- On fail (3194-3195): InvalidOperationException("Could not add report. Payment not verified").
- Again NO `r.get().getStatus()` check. Radiology `report` is overwritable after VERIFIED.

THE STRUCTURED-RESULT (ASYMMETRIC) SIDE — order-status-locked, proving the asymmetry:
- LAB result/level/range/unit are written ONLY by verify_lab_test (3960-3983) and save_lab_test_results (3985-4003). BOTH hard-gate on ORDER status == COLLECTED: verify_lab_test 3968-3970 `if(!t.get().getStatus().equals("COLLECTED")) throw ...("Could not verify, only COLLECTED tests can be verified")`; save_lab_test_results 3993-3995 `...("Could not update, only COLLECTED tests can be saved/updated")`. verify then sets status VERIFIED (3980). After VERIFIED, neither endpoint accepts the order (no longer COLLECTED) → result/level/range/unit are PERMANENTLY FROZEN.
- RADIOLOGY result/attachment are written ONLY by verify_radiology (4272-4295) and save_radiology_results (4297-4315). BOTH hard-gate on ORDER status == ACCEPTED: 4280-4281 `if(!radiology.get().getStatus().equals("ACCEPTED")) throw ...("Could not verify, only ACCEPTED radiologies can be verified")`; 4305-4306 `...("Could not update, only ACCEPTED radiologies can be updated")`. verify then sets VERIFIED (4292). After VERIFIED → result frozen.

NET: same entity, two fields. `result/level/range/unit` (lab) and `result/attachment` (radiology) are immutable post-VERIFIED by an explicit order-status guard; `report` is mutable forever because its endpoint has NO order-status guard. The asymmetry is produced precisely by the ABSENCE of any `getStatus()` check in add_report (3389/3391 lab, 3191/3193 rad) contrasted with its PRESENCE in verify/save (3968, 3993, 4280, 4305). `report` and `result/range/level/unit` are confirmed distinct persisted columns (LabTest.java:47,49,51-54; Radiology.java:46,48,50).

ASSESSMENT — LEGACY DEFECT, not intended design (clinical-safety / amendment-trail concern), recommend NOT reproducing as-is. Evidence it is unintentional rather than a deliberate 'addendum' feature: (1) The guard is copy-pasted from the create/order-time bill check and never adapted for the post-verification edit case — it reuses the same PAID|COVERED|VERIFIED bill-status triple that gates the original order, with no thought given to order lifecycle. (2) No amendment metadata is captured: setReport overwrites in place with NO amendedBy/amendedOn/amendedAt, NO prior-value retention, NO status change — so a post-VERIFIED report edit is silent and untraceable (recall MEMORY: Envers is a phantom dep, NOT active, so there is NO database-level audit catch-net either). (3) The structured-data side was deliberately locked (explicit COLLECTED/ACCEPTED guards with bespoke messages), showing the authors DID care about post-verification immutability for results — the report field was simply missed. (4) A verified clinical report being silently overwritable with no re-verification and no trail is a patient-safety hazard (a corrected/contradictory narrative could be substituted under a finalized, signed result with no record). This is the canonical 'exact process vs legacy defect' fork that the engagement rules say must be escalated to engagement-lead, NOT silently improved and NOT blindly reproduced.

RECOMMENDATION (for engagement-lead change-request decision; do not self-resolve): Raise as ambiguity AMB-INC06A-ITEM4. Recommended target behaviour: treat post-VERIFIED report editing as a controlled AMENDMENT, not a free overwrite — either (a) lock `report` post-VERIFIED symmetrically with result (simplest parity-with-intent), or preferably (b) allow a post-VERIFIED amendment ONLY through an explicit amend path that records amendedBy/amendedOn/amendedAt and retains the prior narrative (append-only addendum), gated by privilege. Until engagement-lead rules, flag the as-built inc-05 LabTest.addReport COLLECTED-only gate as ITSELF a silent (undocumented-as-CR) deviation from legacy that happens to be safer — it must be ratified, not assumed.

As-built inc-05 does NOT reproduce the legacy asymmetry and in fact INVERTS/eliminates the post-VERIFIED report overwrite — this is an unratified divergence that must be surfaced.

LAB (LabTestService.java:400-414): as-built `addReport` gates on ORDER status == COLLECTED (406-409, 'Please collect the lab test first'), NOT on bill status. Consequences vs legacy: (a) report can be written/overwritten ONLY while COLLECTED; once the test is VERIFIED, addReport is REJECTED — the legacy post-VERIFIED overwrite is GONE; (b) the as-built also removed the bill-status gate that legacy used (it relies on the local 'settled' flag set at order time, never re-reading bill status — ADR-0008 §6). So neither legacy guard (bill-status) nor the legacy permissiveness (any-order-status) is preserved: the gate moved from bill→order and from any-status→COLLECTED-only. Strictly speaking the as-built is SAFER but is a silent behavioural change from legacy and is NOT recorded as an approved CR for the report field specifically (the javadoc cites M16 result-flow parity, not an ITEM4 amendment decision).

RADIOLOGY (RadiologyService.java): there is NO standalone addReport endpoint at all. `report` is written ONLY inside verify (ACCEPTED→VERIFIED, 295-312) and never again; saveResult (352-361) writes result only, ACCEPTED-only. So the legacy radiology post-VERIFIED report overwrite path is fully ABSENT in as-built — report becomes immutable at verification. Again safer than legacy, but an unratified divergence.

GAP for inc-06A: (1) Decide and ratify the intended report-amendment policy (legacy permissive overwrite vs as-built COLLECTED-only lock vs proper audited amendment) — currently the as-built silently chose 'lock', which exceeds the 'exact process' mandate without a CR. (2) If any post-VERIFIED amendment is to be allowed, it needs the real-bill-status read seam already flagged for ITEMs 2/4 (the local 'settled' flag cannot reproduce the legacy bill-status gate, and cannot detect a later cash payment flipping the bill to PAID). (3) Whatever is chosen, add amendment metadata (amendedBy/On/At + prior-value retention) since legacy captured none and Envers is a phantom dep (no DB-level trail).

extraction.item: ITEM 5 — Attachment file storage + download (lab + radiology), LEGACY-PARITY LOCAL-DISK
extraction.legacyBehaviour:
UPLOAD (lab: PatientServiceImpl.java:2822-2915; rad: 2921-3014). Endpoints: POST /patients/upload_lab_test_attachment (PatientResource.java:5941-5956) and POST /patients/upload_radiology_attachment (6074-6089), both multipart: @RequestPart("file") MultipartFile + @RequestParam lab_test_id|radiology_id (Long) + @RequestParam name (String). Resource looks up parent by id (404 "Lab test not found" / "Radiology not found"), then delegates to service.

Service flow (identical for both except prefix/parent/status/createdBy source):
1. Count existing attachments via labTestAttachmentRepository.findAllByLabTest / radiologyAttachmentRepository.findAllByRadiology; if size == 5 throw InvalidOperationException "Can not add more than 5 attachments" (2828-2830 / 2927-2929). NOTE: guard is exact-equality ==5, not >=5.
2. Parent-status gate: lab requires status.equals("COLLECTED") else InvalidOperationException "Can only attach for collected tests" (2832-2834); rad requires status.equals("ACCEPTED") else "Can only attach for accepted tests" (2931-2933). Order: count-cap check FIRST, then status check.
3. Size cap: Long maxFileSize = 10485760L (=10 MiB, 10*1024*1024); if file.getSize() > maxFileSize throw InvalidEntryException "File exceeds maximum file size allowed" (2836-2840 / 2935-2939).
4. Storage path = CompanyProfile.publicPath. Loads ALL CompanyProfile rows (companyProfileRepository.findAll()) and keeps the LAST one in the loop (2846-2850 / 2945-2949). Guards: companyProfile==null -> NotFoundException "Company Profile not found"; publicPath==null -> NotFoundException "Driver not found. Contact Administrator" (lab) / "Driver not found. Contact System Administrator" (rad) — wording differs between lab and rad on the null branch; publicPath.equals("") -> "Driver not found. Contact System Administrator" (both) (2852-2860 / 2951-2959).
5. Path path = Paths.get(publicPath); if !Files.exists(path) Files.createDirectories(path) (2863-2867 / 2962-2966).
6. App-generated filename scheme (2873-2877 / 2972-2976): fileRawName = (PREFIX + parent.getId().toString() + parent.getPatient().getNo() + String.valueOf(Math.random()) + LocalDateTime.now().toString()).trim().replace("/","").replace(".","").replace(":","").replace("-",""). PREFIX = "LT" for lab, "RAD" for rad. fileExtension = FilenameUtils.getExtension(file.getOriginalFilename()) (Apache commons-io). fileName = fileRawName + "." + fileExtension. NOTE: the four replace() calls strip / . : - from the raw name (including the dots/colons/dashes of the LocalDateTime and the dot of Math.random()), so the only remaining dot is the one explicitly concatenated before the extension.
7. Persist bytes: Files.copy(file.getInputStream(), path.resolve(fileName), StandardCopyOption.REPLACE_EXISTING) (2879-2882 / 2978-2981).
8. A fileUri is built via ServletUriComponentsBuilder path "/image/" + file.getOriginalFilename() (2884-2887 / 2983-2986) but is DEAD CODE — never returned or stored.
9. Persist DB row: LabTestAttachment/RadiologyAttachment with setName(name), setFileName(fileName) ONLY for the file (no path, no bytes, no extension, no original filename, no size persisted); setLabTest/setRadiology(parent); createdBy = labTest.getCollectedBy() (lab, 2902) / radiology.getAcceptedby() (rad, 3001) — NOT the request user; createdOn = dayService.getDay().getId(); createdAt = dayService.getTimeStamp() (2895-2906 / 2994-3005).
10. Service return value is literally `return null;` on success (2909 / 3008); the resource wraps it as ResponseEntity.created(uri).body(null) (5955 / 6088). On IOException returns ResponseEntity<>(HttpStatus.INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR) (2911-2913 / 3010-3012).

DOWNLOAD (lab: PatientResource.java:5960-6007; rad: 6093-6140). GET /patients/download_lab_test_attachment and /patients/download_radiology_attachment, @RequestParam file_name (String), writes to HttpServletResponse, returns void. Flow (identical both): load all CompanyProfile, keep last; same null/empty publicPath guards (download null-branch message is "Driver not found. Contact System Administrator" for BOTH lab and rad — 5977 / 6110); fileLocation = publicPath; File downloadFile = new File(fileLocation + fileName) — RAW STRING CONCATENATION with NO File.separator between path and filename (5987 / 6120), so publicPath must end with a separator or path resolves incorrectly; byte[] = Files.readAllBytes(downloadFile.toPath()) — whole file into memory via ByteArrayOutputStream; response.setContentType("application/*") (5993 / 6126); response.addHeader("Content-Disposition", "inline; filename=" + fileName) (5995 / 6128) — INLINE disposition (browser preview), filename NOT quoted; writes out to response.getOutputStream(), flush, close; IOException is swallowed via e.printStackTrace() (5997-6005 / 6130-6138). NO status/authorization gate on download in the resource (the only VERIFIED gate is on DELETE, see below). There is NO mapping of attachment-id -> fileName here; the caller passes file_name directly (path-traversal exposure: fileName is concatenated unsanitised — FLAG for security-architect).

DELETE (lab: PatientResource.java:6010-6068; rad: 6143-6177+). POST /patients/delete_lab_test_attachment / delete_radiology_attachment, @RequestParam attachment_id (Long), void. Loads attachment by id (404 "Attachment not found"); if parent.getStatus().equals("VERIFIED") throw InvalidOperationException "Could not delete. Lab Test already verified" (lab 6021-6022) / "Could not delete. Radiology already verified" (rad 6154-6155); else repository.deleteById(attachment_id). DB-only delete — the physical file on disk is NEVER removed (orphaned on disk). Commented-out publicPath block (6028+) confirms file-delete was abandoned.

ENTITIES: LabTestAttachment.java:35-57 and RadiologyAttachment.java:23-50 persist id, name (String, no @NotBlank), fileName (@NotBlank, @Column(unique=true) — globally unique), parent @ManyToOne (EAGER, optional=false, OnDelete NO_ACTION), createdBy/createdOn/createdAt. No bytes, no path, no size, no MIME stored.
states: Lab attachment upload precondition: parent LabTest.status == COLLECTED (else 'Can only attach for collected tests')
Radiology attachment upload precondition: parent Radiology.status == ACCEPTED (else 'Can only attach for accepted tests')
Attachment delete blocked when parent status == VERIFIED ('Could not delete. Lab Test already verified' / 'Could not delete. Radiology already verified')
Download: NO status gate in legacy resource (any caller with the file_name can stream the file)

guards: Upload guard order is FIXED: (1) attachment count == 5 cap, THEN (2) parent-status gate, THEN (3) file.getSize() > 10485760 size cap, THEN (4) CompanyProfile/publicPath resolution. Reproduce in this exact order so the first-failing message matches legacy.

Count cap uses exact equality (size == 5), not >= 5. With the legacy save not being atomic against the count, a 6th concurrent upload could theoretically slip past; the as-built uses canAttach(count) — verify it preserves the == vs >= observable (legacy throws only when count is exactly 5, i.e. allows indices 0..4, max 5 stored).
Size cap = strictly greater-than 10485760L (10 MiB). A file of exactly 10485760 bytes is ACCEPTED (not > cap).

publicPath resolution loads ALL CompanyProfile rows and uses the LAST iterated (no ordering) — assumes a singleton CompanyProfile. Null vs empty-string publicPath produce DIFFERENT messages; the lab UPLOAD null-branch says 'Contact Administrator' while rad upload and BOTH downloads say 'Contact System Administrator' — preserve the per-path wording exactly.

Filename is app-generated, NOT user-supplied: PREFIX(LT/RAD)+parentId+patient.getNo()+Math.random()+LocalDateTime.now(), then strip / . : - , then append '.'+extension(from original filename). createdBy = parent.collectedBy/acceptedBy, NOT the request user.

Download builds File via raw string concat publicPath + fileName (no separator inserted) and streams inline with Content-Disposition 'inline; filename=' + fileName (unquoted). fileName is unsanitised -> path-traversal risk: FLAG to security-architect, but exact-process parity requires the same inline/application-* behaviour; the modern impl should add traversal protection as an explicit, approved deviation, not a silent one.
Delete is DB-row-only; physical file is orphaned on disk in legacy. Decide explicitly whether inc-06A also unlinks the disk file (legacy does NOT) — flag as a deviation if it does.

extraction.asBuiltDelta:
As-built inc-05 stores ONLY metadata refs and has NO file I/O at all — it is a metadata stub, not local-disk parity.

WHAT EXISTS: LabTestAttachment.java / RadiologyAttachment.java (clinical.domain) persist name + fileName (fileName @NotBlank unique, V24 uq_lab_test_attachments_file_name) extending AuditableEntity. LabTestService.addAttachment (LabTestService.java:470-494) and the radiology equivalent enforce: count cap via lt.canAttach(count) with legacy message order preserved (status check first then 'Can not add more than 5 attachments', LabTestService.java:477-485 — NOTE the as-built reorders messages: status 'Can only attach for collected tests' BEFORE cap, matching legacy lines 2833 then 2829, OK). deleteAttachment (520-534) enforces canDeleteAttachment() / VERIFIED gate with message 'Cannot delete attachment from a verified lab test' (DEVIATION: legacy wording is 'Could not delete. Lab Test already verified' — see verbatimMessages; flag for QA exact-string parity). listAttachments returns all rows (LabTestService.java:503-509). Endpoints: POST /lab-tests/uid/{uid}/attachments (JSON @RequestBody LabTestAttachmentRequest{name, fileName}), GET .../attachments (list), DELETE /lab-tests/attachments/uid/{attachmentUid} (LabTestController.java:285-328).

WHAT IS MISSING for ITEM 5 (owner-chosen local-disk parity):
1. No multipart upload — request is JSON with a client-supplied fileName string (LabTestAttachmentRequest.java:17-26). Legacy receives MultipartFile bytes and GENERATES the fileName server-side. Must add a multipart endpoint accepting the file part + name.
2. No 10485760-byte (10 MiB) size cap + 'File exceeds maximum file size allowed' (legacy 2836-2840). Add it (strictly >, exactly 10 MiB accepted).
3. No server-side filename generation. Must implement the exact scheme: 'LT'/'RAD' + parentId + patient.getNo() + Math.random() + LocalDateTime.now(), stripped of /.:- , then '.'+extension(from original filename via FilenameUtils.getExtension). NOTE: as-built clinical does NOT hold patient.getNo() locally per ADR-0008 cross-module rules — establish whether patientNo is available in the clinical aggregate or needs a read seam; the legacy reads parent.getPatient().getNo() directly.
4. No disk write (Files.copy to CompanyProfile.publicPath / configured path) and no directory-create. Owner decision: use a CONFIGURED path (app property) rather than the legacy CompanyProfile.publicPath singleton-row lookup — spec the property and migrate publicPath semantics.
5. No download endpoint at all. Must add GET that streams the file inline: Content-Type per owner choice (legacy = 'application/*'; recommend preserving 'application/*' for bit-parity OR an explicit approved deviation to a correct MIME), Content-Disposition 'inline; filename=' + fileName. RECOMMENDED DEVIATION (requires engagement-lead CR): sanitise fileName against path traversal and quote the filename — legacy does neither (PatientResource.java:5987,5995).
6. No createdBy=collectedBy/acceptedBy nuance — as-built uses AuditableEntity actor; legacy sets createdBy to the parent's collectedBy/acceptedBy user id, not the uploader. Decide whether to preserve (observable in any created-by display).
7. Legacy delete leaves the physical file orphaned on disk; as-built has no disk file to orphan — once disk write is added, decide explicitly whether to also unlink (legacy does NOT).

chunks.title: Bill-status read seam — publish billing.api.BillingQueries.getBillStatus(billUid) (enables C5/C6)
chunks.scope: Add a NEW narrow read-only published interface billing.api.BillingQueries with a single method: BillStatus getBillStatus(String billUid) (throws NotFoundException if the bill uid is unknown). BillStatus is already exposed via the billing::api NamedInterface (BillStatus.java:23). Implementation package-private in billing.application (e.g. BillingQueriesImpl) delegating to PatientBillRepository.findByUid(...).getStatus(); reuse the existing BillQueryService/PatientBillRepository read path. Parameters Strings only; only the already-published BillStatus enum crosses the boundary (ADR-0008 §1 honoured). @Transactional(readOnly=true), propagation REQUIRED (runs in caller tx). This seam is REQUIRED because the legacy add_report gate reads the LIVE PatientBill.status IN {PAID, COVERED, VERIFIED}, and the clinical-local settled flag is provably insufficient (boolean; captured at order time; cash-PAID propagation deferred per RadiologyService.java:73 — a bill paid AFTER order creation still shows settled=false). ADR/cycle impact: this is a clinical->billing READ edge; clinical already depends on billing::api for cancelCharge/recordClinicalCharge, so NO new module edge and NO cycle (billing does NOT depend on clinical). Requires an addendum to ADR-0008 (read seam added) and a note in ADR-0022 confirming clinical->billing::api read is permitted. Document explicitly that this RELAXES ADR-0008 §6 'clinical never reads billing bill-status post-hoc' for the specific, owner-ratified add_report parity case ONLY — a scoped, recorded exception, not a general post-hoc read license. NO Flyway.

chunks.title: ITEM2 — Radiology stand-alone bill-gated add_report (NEW) + lab add_report gate correction

Replace with the SAME bill-status gate (BillingQueries IN {PAID,COVERED,VERIFIED}) and VERBATIM message "Could not add report. Payment not verified", removing the COLLECTED order-status check. Both share the C4 seam. SCOPE BOUNDARY: this chunk reproduces legacy add_report EXACTLY (bill-gated, any order status, free overwrite) — the post-VERIFIED amendment-policy question is ITEM4 (C6) and must be resolved by the engagement-lead CR (AMB-INC06A-ITEM4) BEFORE this chunk's overwrite-after-VERIFIED behaviour is finalized. If the C6 CR lands first and chooses to lock/audit, fold that into this chunk instead of pure legacy overwrite. NO Flyway unless C6 adds amendment columns.

chunks.title: ITEM4 — Post-VERIFIED report-amendment policy: escalate AMB-INC06A-ITEM4, implement per ratified CR
chunks.scope: This is a DECISION-GATED chunk, not free implementation. The legacy add_report has NO order-status guard, so a verified clinical report is silently overwritable forever with NO amendedBy/On/At and NO prior-value retention (Envers is a confirmed phantom dep — no DB audit catch-net). This is a clinical-safety / amendment-trail concern (the canonical exact-process-vs-legacy-defect fork). ACTION via engagement-lead: raise AMB-INC06A-ITEM4 as a change request; do NOT self-resolve. Present three options: (a) reproduce legacy free overwrite at any status (pure parity, unsafe); (b) lock report post-VERIFIED symmetrically with result (parity-with-intent; matches what as-built accidentally did but un-ratified); (c) RECOMMENDED — allow a post-VERIFIED change only via an explicit AMEND path that records amendedBy/amendedOn/amendedAt + retains prior narrative (append-only addendum), privilege-gated. Also flag that the existing as-built COLLECTED-only lock (lab) and radiology report-immutable-at-verify are THEMSELVES un-ratified deviations needing a CR regardless. If option (c)/(b) is chosen, this chunk adds the amendment columns (Flyway V38/V39) + amend endpoint; if (a), this chunk is doc-only and C5's pure-overwrite stands. DEFER actual code until the CR verdict is recorded.

chunks.title: ITEM5 — Attachment local-disk storage + multipart upload + inline download (lab + radiology)
chunks.scope: Owner decision = legacy-parity LOCAL-DISK storage. Replace the as-built JSON metadata stub with real file I/O. UPLOAD: add multipart endpoints POST /lab-tests/uid/{uid}/attachments (multipart: file part + name) and the radiology equivalent. Guard order EXACTLY: (1) count cap exact-equality ==5 -> VERBATIM "Can not add more than 5 attachments"; legacy checks count-then-status but the as-built reorders to status-first (LabTestService.java:477-485) — preserve the legacy FIRST-FAILING message for each branch (status "Can only attach for collected tests" / "Can only attach for accepted tests"); (2) parent-status gate (lab COLLECTED, radiology ACCEPTED); (3) size cap strictly > 10485760L (exactly 10 MiB ACCEPTED) -> VERBATIM "File exceeds maximum file size allowed". Server-side filename generation reproducing legacy scheme: PREFIX("LT"/"RAD") + parentId + patient.getNo() + Math.random() + LocalDateTime.now(), then .replace strip of / . : - , then '.' + FilenameUtils.getExtension(originalFilename). NOTE: clinical does not hold patient.getNo() locally — resolve via the patientUid already on the order plus a registration::api read seam, OR confirm patientNo is available on the clinical aggregate (flag to engagement-lead; may need a small registration read seam — NOT the billing seam). STORAGE PATH: owner-chosen CONFIGURED app property (NOT the legacy CompanyProfile.publicPath singleton-row lookup) — spec the property (e.g. hmis.attachments.base-path); create directories if absent. Persist entity row name+fileName only (no bytes/path/size/MIME). DOWNLOAD: add GET endpoints streaming inline; Content-Type "application/*" (legacy bit-parity) OR approved-deviation correct MIME; Content-Disposition "inline; filename=" + fileName. RECOMMENDED DEVIATIONS (require engagement-lead CR, do NOT do silently): sanitise fileName against path traversal and use File.separator / Path.resolve instead of legacy raw concat (PatientResource.java:5987); quote the filename. DELETE: keep VERIFIED gate but CORRECT the as-built message "Cannot delete attachment from a verified lab test" to VERBATIM legacy "Could not delete. Lab Test already verified" / "Could not delete. Radiology already verified". Legacy delete leaves the disk file orphaned (DB-row-only) — confirm with engagement-lead whether inc-06A also unlinks (legacy does NOT); flag as deviation if it does. createdBy nuance: legacy sets createdBy = parent.collectedBy/acceptedBy (NOT uploader) — confirm whether to preserve (observable in created-by display) or use AuditableEntity actor. Flyway: NONE if entity columns unchanged; if a size/mime/original-name column is added per owner choice, V38.

spec.billStatusSeamDecision: DECISION: Publish a NEW narrow read-only seam in billing.api — interface BillingQueries with a single method `BillStatus getBillStatus(String billUid)` (NotFoundException on unknown uid). The settled flag is NOT sufficient and the rationale is dispositive: (1) the legacy add_report gate (PatientResource.java:3389 lab / :3191 radiology) reads the LIVE PatientBill.status and admits exactly the set {PAID, COVERED, VERIFIED}; the clinical-local settled flag is a boolean that collapses that three-value distinction; (2) settled is captured at ORDER creation (RadiologyService.java:148, isSettledFromCharge:581-587) and the cash-PAID->settled propagation is explicitly DEFERRED (RadiologyService.java:73) — so a bill paid AT THE CASHIER AFTER the order was created still shows settled=false, which would make a settled-flag gate WRONGLY REJECT an add_report that legacy ALLOWS. That is an observable behaviour divergence on a clinical-report write path, unacceptable under exact-process. SHAPE: Strings-in (billUid), already-published BillStatus enum out (BillStatus.java:23 is @NamedInterface(\"api\")), so no NEW billing domain type crosses the boundary and ADR-0008 §1 is honoured. Implementation BillingQueriesImpl package-private in billing.application, @Transactional(readOnly=true), propagation REQUIRED (runs inside the caller's clinical tx), delegating to PatientBillRepository.findByUid(...).getStatus() (reuses the existing BillQueryService/repository read path). CYCLE/ADR IMPACT: this is a clinical->billing::api READ edge; clinical ALREADY depends on billing::api (cancelCharge, recordClinicalCharge), and billing does NOT depend on clinical, so NO new module edge and NO dependency cycle — ApplicationModules.verify() stays green. ADR impact: (a) ADR-0008 addendum documenting the new read seam alongside the command seam; (b) ADR-0008 §6 ('clinical never reads billing bill-status post-hoc') must be EXPLICITLY and NARROWLY RELAXED — record a scoped, owner-ratified exception applying ONLY to the add_report bill-gate parity case (items 2 and 4), NOT a general post-hoc-read license; (c) a one-line note in ADR-0022 confirming clinical->billing::api read is an allowed dependency. The order rows already persist patientBillUid (RadiologyService.java:154; LabTest/Procedure equivalents), so the bill ULID to pass is already available. This seam is shared by C5 (radiology add_report new + lab add_report correction) and, if the C6 CR allows any post-VERIFIED amendment, by C6.
spec.flywayPlan: Existing migration ceiling is V37 (V37__clinical_closure_patient_uid.sql); inc-06A migrations start at V38 as directed. Most chunks need NONE: C1 (delete wiring), C2 (cancel cascade), C3 (rejectComment edit — writes existing reject_comment column), C4 (read seam), C5 (add_report — writes existing report column) are all data/code-only on existing schema, NO Flyway. CONDITIONAL migrations, gated on engagement-lead decisions: (1) C6/ITEM4 — IF the AMB-INC06A-ITEM4 CR chooses an audited post-VERIFIED amendment (recommended option c) or a symmetric lock requiring metadata, add V38__clinical_report_amendment_audit.sql adding report_amended_by / report_amended_on / report_amended_at (and optionally a prior-narrative/addendum column or child table) to lab_tests and radiologies; if the CR chooses pure legacy overwrite (option a) or pure lock with no metadata, NO migration. (2) C7/ITEM5 — base local-disk parity needs NO new column (existing name + unique fileName columns suffice; storage is a configured filesystem path, not DB); add V38 (or V39 if C6 took V38) ONLY if owner elects to persist additional attachment metadata (size / mime / original filename) as an approved enhancement beyond legacy parity. Every conditional migration must be reversible and accompanied by a data-migration-engineer reconciliation note; none drop or transform existing rows.

acceptanceCriteria: C1/ITEM1 LAB: deleting a PENDING lab test whose bill had a RECEIVED payment raises exactly ONE PENDING PatientCreditNote for the FULL bill amount with reference VERBATIM 'Canceled lab test', flips the bill to CANCELED and the payment to REFUNDED (soft, not hard-deleted), detaches the PatientInvoiceDetail and deletes the parent PatientInvoice ONLY if no details remain, and hard-deletes the lab test order row. Golden-master: the credit-note amount equals the bill amount and the number comes from DocumentNumberService.
C1/ITEM1 RADIOLOGY: same as lab with reference VERBATIM 'Canceled radiology'. PROCEDURE: same with reference VERBATIM 'Canceled procedure'.
C1/ITEM1 IDEMPOTENCY: a second delete/cancel attempt on an already-CANCELED bill creates NO second credit note (cancelCharge finds no RECEIVED payment).

C1/ITEM1 GUARD: deleting a non-PENDING lab test/radiology/procedure throws with VERBATIM 'Could not delete, only a PENDING lab test can be deleted' / 'Could not delete, only a PENDING radiology can be deleted' / 'Could not delete, only a PENDING procedure can be deleted' (corrects the current as-built 'Only a pending lab test can be deleted'), and NOTHING is mutated.
C1/ITEM1 NO-CREDIT-NOTE CASE: deleting a PENDING order whose bill has NO RECEIVED payment deletes the order, soft-cancels the bill, and creates NO credit note.

C2/ITEM6 CANCEL CASCADE: canceling a PENDING consultation cancels the consultation bill (reference 'Canceled consultation') AND cascades cancelCharge over every UNSETTLED (settled=false) child lab/radiology/procedure/prescription bill in order lab->radiology->procedure->prescription; a SETTLED child bill is left untouched. Consultation cancel guard verbatim unchanged: 'Could not cancel, only a PENDING consultation can be canceled'.

C2/ITEM6 SIGN-OFF/REFERRAL: deceased sign-off with an uncleared bill is blocked with VERBATIM 'Could not get deceased summary. Patient have uncleared bills.' and referral with VERBATIM 'Could not save. Patient have uncleared <type> bill(s)'; confirm the free-path cascade covers UNPAID->CANCELED on sign-off (test-only if already covered).

C3/ITEM3 EDIT-WHILE-REJECTED: calling save-reject-comment on a REJECTED lab test sets rejectComment and returns success; calling it again overwrites rejectComment (re-callable, no one-shot restriction). Radiology behaves identically.

C3/ITEM3 GUARD: calling save-reject-comment on a non-REJECTED order (any other status incl. null) throws with VERBATIM 'Could not save. Only allowed for rejected tests'. A missing order returns 404 with VERBATIM 'Lab Test not found' / 'Radiology not found'. The find-by-id check precedes the status guard (unknown id yields 404, never the guard message).

C3/ITEM3 NO-BLANK-VALIDATION: a null or empty rejectComment is accepted and persisted when status==REJECTED (legacy parity). HTTP status for the guard failure follows the engagement-lead ruling (recommended 422 per as-built convention; record 409-vs-422 decision).

C4 SEAM: billing.api.BillingQueries.getBillStatus(billUid) returns the live BillStatus for a known bill and throws NotFoundException for an unknown uid; ApplicationModules.verify() passes (no cycle; clinical->billing::api read edge only). A bill paid at the cashier AFTER order creation returns PAID (proving live-status, not the stale settled flag).

C5/ITEM2 RADIOLOGY add_report: with bill status in {PAID,COVERED,VERIFIED}, add_report writes ONLY the report field at ANY order status (PENDING/ACCEPTED/REJECTED/VERIFIED/COLLECTED) with no status change; with any other bill status (UNPAID/NONE/CANCELED) it throws VERBATIM 'Could not add report. Payment not verified'; unknown uid -> 404 VERBATIM 'Radiology not found'.

C5/ITEM2 LAB add_report CORRECTION: lab add_report now gates on bill status in {PAID,COVERED,VERIFIED} (NOT order status COLLECTED) and on failure throws VERBATIM 'Could not add report. Payment not verified' (replacing the as-built 'Please collect the lab test first'). Writes only the report field, no status change.

C6/ITEM4: AMB-INC06A-ITEM4 is raised to engagement-lead with the three options and recommendation; NO post-VERIFIED overwrite/lock/audit behaviour is merged until the CR verdict is recorded in an ADR/CR. If audited-amendment is chosen, amend writes report_amended_by/on/at and retains the prior narrative; the structured result/level/range/unit (lab) and result/attachment (radiology) remain frozen post-VERIFIED.

C7/ITEM5 UPLOAD: a 10485760-byte (exactly 10 MiB) file is ACCEPTED; a 10485761-byte file throws VERBATIM 'File exceeds maximum file size allowed'. A 6th attachment throws VERBATIM 'Can not add more than 5 attachments' (cap is exact ==5; 0..4 allowed, max 5 stored). Lab upload on a non-COLLECTED order throws VERBATIM 'Can only attach for collected tests'; radiology on a non-ACCEPTED order throws VERBATIM 'Can only attach for accepted tests'. First-failing message matches legacy ordering.

C7/ITEM5 FILENAME: stored filename matches the legacy scheme (PREFIX LT/RAD + parentId + patientNo + random + datetime, stripped of / . : - , then '.'+extension); the file is written to the configured base path; the DB row persists name + unique fileName only.

C7/ITEM5 DOWNLOAD: GET streams the stored bytes with Content-Disposition 'inline; filename=' + fileName; Content-Type per the ratified choice. A path-traversal fileName is rejected/neutralised (approved deviation), not blindly concatenated.

C7/ITEM5 DELETE: deleting an attachment whose parent is VERIFIED throws VERBATIM 'Could not delete. Lab Test already verified' / 'Could not delete. Radiology already verified' (corrects the as-built 'Cannot delete attachment from a verified lab test'); otherwise the DB row is removed.

CROSS-CUTTING: every chunk ends with 'mvn clean verify' GREEN (including ApplicationModules.verify() and Testcontainers integration tests) and a commit. No business rule, numbering scheme, pricing/insurance logic, or report output changes outside an approved CR.
risks: ADR-0008 §6 relaxation risk: introducing a clinical->billing bill-status READ seam reverses a stated principle ('clinical never reads billing bill-status post-hoc'). MITIGATION: scope the exception narrowly to the add_report parity case, record it in an ADR-0008 addendum + CR; do NOT let it become a general post-hoc read pattern (e.g. settlement gates must keep using the local settled flag).

ITEM4 patient-safety risk: pure legacy parity (option a) preserves a defect where a finalized/signed clinical report can be silently overwritten with no trail (Envers is a confirmed phantom dep — no DB catch-net). MITIGATION: do not merge C5's overwrite-after-VERIFIED until AMB-INC06A-ITEM4 is ruled; recommend the audited-amendment option (c).

ITEM4/ITEM5 silent-divergence risk: the as-built ALREADY deviates from legacy un-ratified (lab addReport COLLECTED-lock; radiology report immutable at verify; attachment delete message; lab delete-guard message). These exceed the exact-process mandate without a CR. MITIGATION: each correction in C1/C5/C7 must be ratified, and the safer pre-existing locks must be explicitly recorded as approved deviations, not assumed.

ITEM5 cross-module data risk: legacy filename generation reads patient.getNo() directly, but clinical does not hold patientNo locally (ADR-0008 boundary). MITIGATION: resolve via a registration::api read seam or confirm patientNo on the clinical aggregate BEFORE C7; if a registration seam is needed, verify it creates no cycle (registration already depends on clinical::api — a clinical->registration edge WOULD cycle; prefer passing patientNo at order time or a shared query interface).

ITEM5 security risk: local-disk parity reproduces a path-traversal exposure (unsanitised fileName raw-concatenated) and inline application/* disposition. MITIGATION: path-traversal hardening + File.separator/Path.resolve as an explicit approved deviation reviewed by security-architect; do not ship the raw-concat verbatim.

HTTP-status divergence risk (ITEM3, and 409-vs-422 generally): legacy InvalidOperationException -> 409; as-built -> 422. Choosing one changes an observable wire contract. MITIGATION: get an explicit engagement-lead ruling and record it; default to as-built 422 for consistency unless byte-exact parity is required.

Concurrency risk (ITEM5 cap): legacy count==5 cap is non-atomic against save; a concurrent 6th upload could slip past. MITIGATION: preserve the observable ==5 semantics but consider a DB-level guard/unique constraint; note legacy itself was racy (do not silently 'fix' the observable without a CR).

Data-continuity risk: any conditional Flyway (C6 amendment columns / C7 extra attachment metadata) must be reversible with a data-migration-engineer reconciliation note; 3 years of production data must migrate losslessly. MITIGATION: gate any column add behind the relevant CR and require reconciliation sign-off before merge.

Scope-creep risk: prescription delete divergences and the switch_to_consultation NONE->UNPAID activation are tempting to fold in. MITIGATION: keep them deferred in the ambiguity register; inc-06A is a SHORT top-up — only pull them in on explicit engagement-lead direction.
